codeReview.summary: The runtime message split itself preserves the schema shape, but the feature still fails review because its runtime-message coverage is not actually comprehensive and the feature commits also include unrelated version bumps.

issues.description: This feature range bumps the package version from 0.5.0 to 0.5.2 even though the work is only about consolidating shared runtime message types. Release metadata changes are outside this feature's scope.
The Chrome extension manifest version is also bumped from 0.5.0 to 0.5.2 as part of this refactor. That is unrelated shipped metadata and should not be bundled into this shared-type change.
The Firefox manifest version is bumped from 0.5.0 to 0.5.2 in the same feature range. This is another release-scoped side effect unrelated to consolidating runtime messages.

The serialization test only samples 18 of the 22 runtime message variants. `assistant_response`, `report_image_captured`, `report_images_selection`, and `session_tabs_update` are present in `packages/shared/src/runtime-messages.ts:80-127` but never exercised here, so the feature does not meet its 'comprehensive unit tests' / 'all message variants serialize-deserialize correctly' requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state docs are stale for runtime messages: they still point reviewers/workers at `runtime-message-definitions.ts` and say there are 21 variants, while the codebase now uses the split `runtime-messages*.ts` structure and the worker transcript itself counted 22 variants.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:31-32 and :95-96 still reference `runtime-message-definitions.ts` and 21 variants; validation-contract.md:10 also says 21 variants; current `packages/shared/src/runtime-messages.ts:80-127` defines 22 variants.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature handoff's `commitId` does not identify the substantive implementation commit, which makes later review harder and obscures what actually changed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff file records `commitId` `57fb7ff` (/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-10T23-22-27-573Z__shared-consolidate-runtime-messages__21b621a2-6b41-4504-8723-8687be69fa76.json:6), but `git show --stat 57fb7ff` only shows version bumps plus deletion of `runtime-message-definitions.ts`, while `git log --oneline -- packages/shared/src/runtime-messages.ts packages/shared/src/runtime-messages-base.ts packages/shared/src/runtime-messages-extended.ts packages/shared/src/runtime-types.ts` identifies `bea2415` as the commit that actually added the new modular runtime-message files and test runner update.

summary: Review failed. The runtime-message refactor mostly preserves the shared schema, but the feature still misses coverage for four declared message variants and bundles unrelated version bumps into the same feature range.
